{{Quote|The command ship ''Terror'' is en-route to Imdaar, where it will likely deploy a full wing of the phantom V-38s.|Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], during a mission briefing|audio=Ackbar2-RAII.flac|<ref name="RAII" />}}
The '''''Terror''''' was an [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] in service to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Under the command of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Sarn]], the ''Terror'' was assigned to protect the development of a new [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]] for use with the [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE Phantom]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] and was itself fitted with one of the devices, allowing it to become invisible to both [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] and the naked [[Eye/Legends|eye]]. During a visit from the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] to oversee the deployment of the new starfighter in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the ''Terror'' was infiltrated by two [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] saboteurs. The infiltrators, [[Rookie One]] and [[Ru Murleen]], managed to steal a TIE Phantom and used it to destroy the ''Terror''{{'s}} [[Reactor/Legends|main reactor]] while it was docked to the [[Imdaar Alpha research station|TIE Phantom factory]] over [[Imdaar Alpha]]. [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Vader escaped the ''Terror'' moments before the reactor exploded, destroying the [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]], but he executed Sarn for his incompetence before leaving.

==Description==
{{Quote|It's darker than Sarlacc's belly down here.|Rookie One on the ''Terror''{{'s}} sewers|audio=Terror1-RAII.flac|<ref name="RAII" />}}
At nineteen [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] long,<ref name="DB" /> the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]]<ref name="SSD" /> ''Terror'' was one of the largest [[Battleship/Legends|battleships]] in service to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Like all those of its class, the ''Terror''{{'s}} [[Color/Legends|gray]] [[Hull/Legends|hull]] was roughly shaped like an [[Arrow/Legends|arrowhead]] when viewed from above. The top of the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] had a smooth hull surrounding an island of habitable sections in the center and a trapezoidal command tower towards the rear. The underside was covered by a network of superstructure and the thirteen red-glowing [[Engine/Legends|engines]] which propelled it through [[Realspace/Legends|space]].<ref name="DB" />

[[File:Epsilon_four.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The ''Terror'' decloaks.]]
When the ''Terror'' was assigned to the [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE Phantom]] project, it was fitted with a [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]]<ref name="RAII" /> powered by [[Stygium/Legends|stygium]] [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]].<ref name="Insider66" /> The cloaking device allowed the ''Terror'' to become invisible to both the naked [[Eye/Legends|eye]] and [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] systems on other starships. Approaching Imperial vessels were required to transmit the correct security code before the ship would decloak to allow them to dock. As the cloaking device was deactivated, the ''Terror'' would become visible from bow to stern, with the whole process completing in under ten [[Standard second|seconds]].<ref name="RAII" />

The ''Terror'' was equipped to act as a [[Carrier/Legends|carrier]] for the Empire's [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of TIE Phantoms. A series of adjoining service [[Hangar/Legends|hangars]], separated by [[Blast door/Legends|blast doors]], contained hundreds of the cloaking device-equipped [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] mounted on racks along the walls. Mechanical [[Limb/Legends|arms]] were used to remove each TIE Phantom from its rack ready to launch or undergo maintenance. The fighters could be moved around the ship suspended from ceiling-mounted rails which ran through cavernous rooms crisscrossed by walkways and retractable bridges. Additional hangars were located on both sides of the ship to allow access for [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]] and other vessels. In addition to the TIE Phantoms stationed aboard the ''Terror'', at least one of these hangars had facilities to store a number of [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]] mounted from its ceiling.<ref name="RAII" />

The ''Terror''{{'s}} [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]], situated in the command tower, featured two crew pits separated by a walkway leading to the large forward [[Viewport/Legends|viewports]]. The ship was fitted with a [[Darth Vader's meditation chamber/Legends|meditation chamber]] for use by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].<ref name="RAII" /> This pressurized spherical chamber contained equipment to allow the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] to quickly remove and replace his [[Darth Vader's armor/Legends|helmet]].<ref name="DB2">{{DB|technology|meditationchamber|meditation chamber}}</ref> The [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] had its own sewer system, comprising a network of interconnected tunnels in which waste material ran beneath walkways. The sewers could be entered via access panels in the floor of the above deck.<ref name="RAII" />

The ship's [[Reactor/Legends|main reactor]] was located in a circular chamber surrounded by [[beam focuser]]s which prevented it from going critical. The reactor could be accessed through a series of large tunnels throughout the superstructure. The tunnels, which were filled with moving machinery and periodically divided by [[Force field/Legends|force fields]] powered by [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]], were large enough for a starfighter to fly through. At least one such tunnel from the reactor core led outside the ship.<ref name="RAII" />

==History==
{{Quote|Shoot out the beam focusers. The reactor should go critical and blow, taking this ship and all these blasted fighters with it.|Ru Murleen|audio=Terror2-RAII.flac|<ref name="RAII" />}}
The ''Terror'' was one of several [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyers]] constructed by the Galactic Empire during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]<ref name="EGW" /> and entered service under the command of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Sarn]]<ref name="Insider66" /> by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].<ref name="EGW" /> For some [[Standard year/Legends|years]], [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Martio Batch]] had been working to develop a practical cloaking device at his [[Imdaar Alpha research station|research facility]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Imdaar Alpha]]. Cloaking devices had once been common, powered by stygium crystals [[Mining/Legends|mined]] from [[Aeten II/Legends|Aeten II]] in the [[Dreighton Nebula/Legends|Dreighton Nebula]]. After the mines ran dry, the technology fell out of use until Batch used the Empire's [[Tarkin (superweapon)|''Tarkin'']] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] to [[Destruction of Aeten II|destroy]] Aeten II, releasing thousands of stygium crystals into space. Batch set about using the crystals to produce a fleet of cloaked starfighters<ref name="Insider66" />—modified [[V38 assault fighter]]s known as TIE Phantoms.<ref name="RAII" /> The Grand Admiral requisitioned the ''Terror''<ref name="Insider66" /> to serve as [[Flagship/Legends|command ship]] of a fleet comprising at least two [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]]<ref name="RAII" /> protecting the project.<ref name="Insider66" /> The Star Dreadnought was itself fitted with a cloaking device and equipped to serve as a carrier for the TIE Phantoms.<ref name="RAII" />

[[File:Imdaar_Alpha.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The ''Terror''{{'s}} destruction]]
Following the Imperial victory over the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] at the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in 3 ABY,<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the Dark Lord of the Sith Darth Vader visited the ''Terror'' to oversee the final field tests of the new starfighters. Vader observed from aboard the Star Dreadnought as TIE Phantoms from [[Sigma Squadron (TIE/ph Phantom)|Sigma Squadron]] intercepted and destroyed a [[Flight group/Legends|flight group]] of Rebel [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] patrolling the region of space known as the [[Dreighton Triangle]]. When the field tests were complete, the Dark Lord ordered Sarn to take the ''Terror'' back to Imdaar Alpha to begin the final preparations to launch the TIE Phantom fleet against the Rebellion.<ref name="RAII" />

The Rebel Alliance learned of the Empire's plans and dispatched [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilots]] [[Ru Murleen]] and [[Rookie One]] to [[Imdaar/Legends|Imdaar]] to [[Mission to Imdaar|infiltrate]] the ''Terror'' and steal a TIE Phantom for study. As the ''Terror'' arrived at Imdaar and prepared to dock with Batch's cloaked facility, the Rebels approached in a [[Epsilon Four|supply shuttle]] stolen from the [[Imdaar landing platform|supply platform]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] and were permitted to dock. The intruders were soon discovered but used the ''Terror''{{'s}} sewer to evade capture and reach a service hangar where they boarded a TIE Phantom. The Imperials quickly closed the outer bay doors to prevent their escape, but the Rebel pilots flew the stolen fighter through the Star Dreadnought's superstructure to its main reactor, destroying the beam focusers surrounding it before making their escape. As the ''Terror''{{'s}} reactor began to overload, Vader [[Death/Legends|executed]] Sarn for his failure before fleeing the doomed Star Dreadnought. The reactor blew soon after, destroying the ''Terror''. The explosion caused the TIE Phantom factory to become visible, allowing the Rebels to fly inside and destroy it, bringing the TIE Phantom project to an end.<ref name="RAII" />

==Commanders and crew==
[[File:SarnVader.png|thumb|left|200px|Admiral Sarn and Darth Vader on the ''Terror''{{'s}} bridge]]
During its involvement with the TIE Phantom project, the ''Terror'' was commanded by Admiral Sarn under the overall command of Grand Admiral Martio Batch, one of the most senior [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]].<ref name="Insider66" /> As the field testing of the new starfighters neared completion, the Dark Lord of the Sith Darth Vader assumed overall command of the project, operating from aboard the ''Terror''. The Star Dreadnought's crew included numerous [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and [[TIE pilot|pilots]], along with various models of [[Droid/Legends|droid]], including [[MSE-6 series repair droid/Legends|MSE-6-series repair droids]], [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droids]] and [[Remote/Legends|remotes]].<ref name="RAII" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Terror'' was created by [[Vincent Lee]] for the [[1995]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]''.<ref name="RAII" /> The ''Terror'' is only described as a "command ship" in the game and is identified only as a Super Star Destroyer in later sources. However, ''The Essential Guide to Warfare'' identifies this as a term covering several classes of vessel and is one of several sources to identify ships of the ''Terror''{{'s}} design as ''Executor''-class Star Dreadnoughts.<ref name="SSD" /><ref name="RAII" />

==Appearances==
